item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Noting that the original picture is visible by opening up the "Files" section at the bottom of the page, and can be seen here.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: It saddens me that I didn't get to see that picture. Based on the opinions I've read it seemed to be something really special.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This SCP is very poor without an image. Any news on the new image?

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Too bad, this SCIp loses context without the picture

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Image could not be reliably sourced, as it mostly leads to a bunch of dead domains, and as such is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0.

A replacement may be made soon due to author sentimentality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
try our new demon flesh

Absolutely not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love articles that are brief but packed with personality and curiosity. This is brilliant. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i mean, im all for the writer making it open ended for the reader to think "why was it padlocked"
i just think, if something came outta that hole, someone would mention it in the file, give it a dash 2915 des and shit, ya know?
thats just my only problem with that line, but i do agree, it was pretty cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
man, you tried to get one by me, but nope
this is in Canton, Ohio

what else can it be

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I love when something can get some creep going without having to go on for hours.

https://youtu.be/JqZbXBTjtCo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "The freezer is to remain padlocked" is still one of my favorite lines in a SCP's containment procedures. It just says so much with so little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So if you fall down the hole when no one is looking or has cameras watching you do you go to hell?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When it mentioned Frank Sinatra, all I thought about was MASH and Trapper John singing that song while operating on someone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Djoric is rather inactive at the moment. You are certainly not the only one advocating for more content. You (and anyone else, really) are free to write up Tales and other articles about this SCP. That is also part of the expansion package, so to speak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't around for that contest, but I still would love to see this SCP expanded upon.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Ah. Thank you for the clarification! I guess even opinions seem "spammy" to me… Haha!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
DrStranger, to clarify, necro-posting is an issue when one makes a post without adding something new to the table (essentially spam).

In that case, the post is merely expressing their opinion and is not really necro-posting (ergo, they could easily comment without replying to Djoric's author post… which could give off the connotation of necro-posting).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think it needs to be expanded. I mean, this is intended to be for a contest for short articles. If you expand on it and do an essay with a word count in thousands, it diminishes the value of the article and the purpose in which the article was written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey there. Now, I ain't staff, but you really shouldn't be replying to posts made more than a year ago. If Djoric has any plans for this scip, I'm pretty sure they would've implemented them by now.

E: And keep in mind, this was an entry for the Short Works Contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should expand this. It is a great SCP, only brought down by its shortness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as I love long, winding articles, short and effective pieces like these are what got me interested in the wiki seven years ago. An enthusiastic +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meh, Just some demon stuf-
Never mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So let's see….. *looks at checklist* Demon flesh? Check. Meat hooks? Check. Poison gas? Check. Unidentified poison gas? Check. Evil freezer? Check. Comes out at night? Check. Bottomless pits that most likely lead to hell? Check. ….Frank Sinatra? Uh, check, I guess.

But seriously, this was a really well done SCP. And the Frank Sinatra bit really made this for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, the picture and the hole part make me think that the hole leads to hell. It's scary because I'm stigiophobic. (That's the phobia of hell)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is still one of my favorite things to come out of the short story contest.
So much utter wrongness…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is totally just me defending Djoric-senpai's honor in the hopes that he will Notice Me after His Glorious Return, but think of it this way:

  • A Wendy's starts selling weird food that really, really oughtn't be on the menu. Customers assume it's a novelty act and start eating there more frequently than before, eventually becoming well-known enough to grab the Foundation's attention.
  • The Foundation shuts the place down under the guise of a failed health inspection or summat.
  • The Foundation reopens it as a front company, allowing them to study the anomaly, earn some dough, and convince the town that nothing crazier than "roadkill on the menu" was happening, all in one fell swoop.

The alternative is to treat the building like it's condemned or otherwise draw more attention to the building than the Foundation wants to draw. The way it's written now, conspiracies and public suspicion are kept to an absolute minimum; the truth is far crazier than anything anyone would ever suspect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm willing to relax and laugh at an SCP for once, but there's one issue that puts this firmly into lolfoundation territory and just turns me off to it. As mentioned by others, why is this place still an active functioning restaurant? It would make more sense just to secure, contain, and protect it, without the interference of a bunch of obnoxious customers, if there's no reason to keep operating it as a Wendy's - and from the article, it seems there's absolutely no reason for them to do so.

Suggestions: change the tenor of the containment procedures to make it clear that operating the building as a functioning restaurant is an absolutely necessary part of containment, and include some indication of why this is a requirement. It can be hilarious, but it should at least make sense. Without some duct tape over this gaping plot hole, I feel compelled to downvote. Sorry…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You get my vote. Now if you don't mind, I'm hungry for some meat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so great, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I work fulltime nights at a fast food place, so I really appreciate this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that this is more like what we like to imagine series one stuff was. Which is awesome as hell.

Two months late to the party, but this is fantastic and super-evocative. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. It's so awesomely creepy, and the Frank Sinatra bit just makes it ever more so for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What gets me is that they still run the Wendy's, just under Psi-25.

item.title: Re: Yes expand....this is a real deal!!
item.content:encoded:
Nah. After further thought, I'm going to keep it as is. The value of the short scip is somewhat rare, nowadays.

Also, that is six exclamation points there. You may have a case of the mind-worms. Please see your local wizzard to get your head checked out.

item.title: Yes expand....this is a real deal!!
item.content:encoded: You have really quiver my sense. It was like reading Stephen King…PLEASE EXPAND, just like KholetteDrake said, we have a gem!!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a Wendy's that obtains its meat from Hell, via a convenient hole in the freezer that leads down under. For some reason, Sinatra songs sometimes echo through the hole to the surface.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't…quite understand this SCP /:|

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to disagree with the comments that want this article expanded. I think it's excellent just the way it is. There's enough to get the weirdness across, but not much more and I think that really works here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I so want to see this expanded. And that Weird Al reference…

You got me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the way how this article leaves many questions unanswered, like in the Stairwell one. The efficient simplicity in it makes this one of my favorite articles in the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's got a nice classic SCP feel to it (probably the short length), I like it, but not enough to upvote. Just didn't entertain me much or strike me as very original.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, the quality standards for SCP's are much higher; as in, there are quality standards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree that this should be expanded now that we are post-contest, perhaps a test document about the demon meat, also maybe a tale or something to give a credible reason why the restaurant is still open to the public despite it being an Euclid SCP.

Still, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, OK. Mostly a matter of format, then. Thanks to everyone for examples and explanations!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all, that image is so great that if the article were just that, and an image of you giving me the middle finger, I would probably still have upvoted.

But I really like the article. It's effective; there's no extraneous elements, and the article has just enough information to not leave us feeling shortchanged while still making us want more. I appreciate the added dimension of it being short because what little we're given is what little the Foundation actually knows, which creates mystery.

Then there's the wonderful juxtaposition of the absurdity of MTF Agents working at a Wendy's with a pit that leads to Hell.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if you're asking philosophically or just structurally.

Honestly, my answers are kind of the same for both. Format, clinical tone as opposed to first-person retelling, authorship credit, and location on a central site rather than being copy-pasted around the internet.

The shared semi-canon helps, but I figure those are the fundamental differences. You can still use all the same horror mechanisms, even the one implying you're next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Angry catgirls and sad Russian bugmonsters, mostly.

Also, sad bowls of soup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well it wouldn't be very mysterious if you knew the answer to that, and most likely any answer I could give would be insufficient. It's playing up disparity between what is expected and what is present to create horror - the song itself is of the kind that can imply a secret, horrible meaning (what exactly is under their skin?) but in withholding that information, I let the reader come to their own images.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Outstanding. This captures the spirit of the contest quite well, and is a great exercise in shorter format storytelling. Yes, there are several plot holes and logical inconsistencies as pointed out above, but the mark of a good piece of fiction is that the story is good enough that those things don't matter.

As an example, the idea of a guy whose job it is is to go work at a Wendy's at night so that he can put on a gas mask, shine UV lights on things and then be creeped the fuck out the rest of the time by the locked room in the back is great. What makes it great is that the writer has supplied the necessary tools and setting for the reader to come up with stuff like that after thinking about it.

There's even a measure of silliness to it, but again, the deftness of the writing makes that not really matter. This is one of those articles where the most relevant personal preference becomes "good writing." This is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, but I don't get the Sinatra reference. Everyone seems to love it, but I don't know why that song specifically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP started as creepypasta, but all SCP objects aren't creepypasta now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmh mmh. <nods wisely>

Okay, this seems to be the right time to ask:

What IS the difference between an SCP and a creepypasta?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't at my last visit, but I have to do it now: excellent joke, Ihpkmn! <VBG>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Explanation in a few words: There wouldn't be a story otherwise.

Another explanation in a few words: MTF guys working at a Wendy's is funny.

Explanation in more than a few words: Creepypasta is essentially the written form of a campfire story - it works better the less exacting you are when you think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted immediately after reading the sentence about Sinatra.

Not sure this should be expanded… It's perfect as is. Oh no, wait - I do agree that it'd be good to know why the restaurant is still open… Why not close it and be done with it? And I don't think this can be done in a few words!

The drone reported for four months… Wow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's also possible that they renovated and installed a second freezer. Or maybe they just have a fleet of refrigerator trucks servicing the Wendy's 24/7. It's the Foundation, it could honestly be anything.

I'm pretty confident the freezer was installed by someone like Richard and Sons. But not by them, of course. They're strictly local.

(I haven't voted on this one yet, but that's entirely because I keep reading it while exhausted and my opinions get weird. Hopefully soon I'll give it an honest shot.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty hilarious, but I noticed it doesn't have a title on the Series page

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a Slenderman-mythos webseries called "EverymanHYBRID", in which a group of young men making a series about exercise and health tips accidentally stumble into the eldritch. One of the things they find is an apparently immortal demonic spirit known as HABIT, which possesses one of their number. Early on, one of the signs of his possession was the fact that whenever he got in his car, the radio would start to play "I've Got You Under My Skin".

Given some of the things poor Evan was forced to do later, that song really creeps me out now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are several sites that let you add your own text to photos of random signage. I wouldn't be surprised if that was the case for this sign.
